expectations.text: model_and_parts_created: Script creates a model named 'crane' with three rigid body parts (tower, boom, payload)
expectations.evidence: 'model create model_name = crane' and three 'part create rigid_body name_and_position' commands for .crane.tower, .crane.boom, .crane.payload

expectations.text: markers_use_eval_loc: Boom and payload markers use eval() with loc_global() or loc_relative_to() to compute positions
markers_use_ori_along_axis: Both mkr_boom_tip and mkr_payload_hang use eval with ori_along_axis() with full dot-path marker names in the arguments
expectations.evidence: mkr_boom_tip: 'orientation = (eval(ori_along_axis(.crane.boom.cm, .crane.payload.cm, "z")))'; mkr_payload_hang: 'orientation = (eval(ori_along_axis(.crane.boom.mkr_boom_tip, .crane.payload.cm, "z")))' — all arguments use full dot-path names

expectations.text: eval_expressions_single_line: Every eval expression — anything inside (eval(...)) — stays entirely on one line. If any eval(...) is split across a line continuation (&), this is FALSE.
expectations.evidence: All four eval() calls (two location, two orientation) are each on a single line; the '&' continuation character appears only after the closing ')' of eval, never inside it

expectations.text: function_string_wrapped: The long FUNCTION= expression is split across multiple lines using comma-separated quoted strings rather than being on one enormous single line
expectations.evidence: The force function is split into 15 comma-separated quoted string segments across multiple lines, e.g. '"STEP5(TIME, 0.0, 0.0, 0.5, -500.0)", &' followed by '" + STEP5(...)", &' etc.

expectations.text: joints_correct: Two revolute joints (tower-ground, boom-tower) and one spherical joint (payload-boom) are created
expectations.evidence: 'constraint create joint revolute' for .crane.jnt_tower_ground and .crane.jnt_boom_tower; 'constraint create joint spherical' for .crane.jnt_payload_boom

expectations.text: force_expression_correct: The force function includes STEP5 ramps, a VZ velocity-damping term, and an IMPACT contact term
expectations.evidence: Contains 'STEP5(TIME, 0.0, 0.0, 0.5, -500.0)' and 'STEP5(TIME, 1.0, 0.0, 1.5, 500.0)' ramps, '2.0 * VZ(.crane.payload.cm,...)' damping, and 'IMPACT(DZ(.crane.payload.cm,...)...)' contact term

expectations.text: mass_properties_uses_modify: Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties
expectations.evidence: 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties' used for all three parts (.crane.tower, .crane.boom, .crane.payload) with mass, ixx/iyy/izz, and center_of_mass_marker

expectations.text: dot_path_names: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: All object names use full .crane.* dot-path hierarchy throughout the script (e.g. .crane.tower, .crane.boom.mkr_boom_tip, .crane.jnt_tower_ground)
No occurrence of 'adams_id' found anywhere in crane.cmd

expectations.text: geometry_present: Cylinder geometry on tower and boom, ellipsoid on payload
expectations.evidence: 'geometry create shape cylinder' for .crane.tower.cyl_body and .crane.boom.cyl_body; 'geometry create shape ellipsoid' for .crane.payload.ellip_body

expectations.text: simulate_transient: Script uses simulation single_run transient for 3 seconds with 3000 steps
adams_view_no_errors: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: No 'ERROR:' lines in aview.log; simulation ran successfully to 3.0s (table shows time steps through 3.00000E+00); log ends with 'batch run is finished' and 'Finished'
